commit.message:
results: 1.00 — run 9 with fixed bias penalties, cost -7.41

Fixed PMOS/NMOS threshold mismatch in bias checks removed constant
+300 false penalty. DE now gets meaningful bias feedback. Cost dropped
from ~292 to -7.41. All 6/6 specs met: 174dB gain, 26.5MHz GBW,
29.6V swing, 307mW, 168dB CMRR.

commit.message:
fix: Resolve bare filenames relative to cwd in shell

Previously only ./foo and ../foo were resolved. Now any argument not
starting with / or - is resolved relative to cwd, so `cat file` works
the same as `cat ./file`.

Initial commit - Native Nomads V2 Wix project

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix recurring event form — use datetime-local inputs throughout

Replace type="date" and type="time" inputs (which caused "Invalid
value" browser validation errors) with datetime-local inputs that
already work. Recurring mode now uses "First Start", "First End",
and "Repeat Until" datetime-local fields. The time-of-day is
extracted from the first occurrence and applied to all generated
events in the series.

commit.message:
feat: implement VMM Batch 9 — 16 audit functions (V-207510–V-207525)

Implement 16 CAT II audit-related check functions for the XCP-ng VMM
module. Each function verifies auditd is active and checks for specific
audit rules covering security levels, privileged functions, concurrent
sessions, logon events, VMM access, and module loading.

Also revises the Vates compliance report: rephrases classified/
unclassified references and adds CAT I/II/III severity descriptions
for the Vates audience.

Module progress: 177/193 explicit functions (91.7%), 16 stubs remain.
Test185/Test186 validated: 0 errors, 0 VulnTimeouts, EvalScore 32.12%.

fix(recorder-bot): bypass domain filter when organizer email unresolved

When getMeetingDetails() fails, the organizer_email stays as the
'pending-lookup' placeholder. The domain filter then rejects it since
it has no @ sign, silently dropping legitimate meetings. Now bypasses
the filter when the email is clearly unresolved, logging a warning
instead.

fix: replace hardcoded dark theme colors with dynamic useTheme() colors in 8 screen files

StyleSheet.create() blocks and into inline style overrides using the dynamic colors
from useTheme(). Brand colors (Colors.primary, Colors.gold, etc.) left unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Close sorry (12) Gauss's law, decompose sorry (7) UniformSchwartzDecay (3→2)

- Proved hGauss: divergence of E=0 simplifies to 0, simp closes ∫eM=ρ_ion
- Decomposed hSchwartz: proved hGradDecay (spatial constant → gradient 0),
  left hDecay sorry (needs iteratedFDeriv polynomial×Gaussian bound)
- Updated MEMORY.md, docstrings, critique, plan, log

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): corrigir redirecionamento após publicar artefato

O redirect após criar um artefato ia para /{locale}/{scope}/{name}
que não existe. A rota correta é /{locale}/a/{scope}/{name}.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct bias point penalties — PMOS/NMOS threshold mismatch

The bias checks were using NMOS thresholds (VTO=0.7) to check PMOS
devices (M1, M5) and PMOS thresholds (VTO=-0.7) to check NMOS device
(M6a). This added a constant +300 false penalty to all evaluations,
hiding meaningful bias point feedback from DE. Fix uses correct
threshold for each device type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Supabase 1000-row default limit on crawler stats API

Added fetchAllRows() pagination helper that fetches all matching rows
in pages of 1000. Applied to summary, pages, sessions, and spider-web
views which were previously truncated at 1000 results.

commit.message:
feat: Add sys_kill, sys_task_info syscalls, ps command, and kill builtin

Kernel:
- SYS_TASK_KILL (104): terminate a task by TID (requires CAP_TASK_MGMT or same UID)
- SYS_TASK_INFO (105): query task state, UID, and parent TID for enumeration

User-space:
- New ps command: lists all running tasks with TID, state, UID, PPID
- Shell kill builtin: `kill <tid>` to terminate tasks

commit.message:
feat: lock accounts to first device using browser fingerprint

- FingerprintJS generates a stable visitor ID on the login page
- First login: fingerprint stored in users.device_fingerprint (Supabase)
- Subsequent logins: fingerprint must match or 403 is returned
- Admin (cloud99) is exempt from device locking
- POST /api/auth/reset-device (admin only): clears fingerprint so user
  can re-register from a new phone
- supabase-users.sql: adds device_fingerprint column

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: le-guide
repository.description: C'est une bibliothèque invisible pour le nomade digital. Elle prend le chaos de vos papiers, utilise l'IA pour les trier et nettoyer, et les sauvegarde dans le cloud pour que vous n'ayez rien à porter physiquement. C'est votre Baguette de l'Étudiant : vous n'avez même pas besoin de la tenir en main, il vous suffit d'exister.

commit.message:
results: 1.00 — run 8, 188dB gain, 25.9MHz GBW, 291mW, cost 292.27

Extended L1/L3 ranges and lower Ibias min. Highest gain yet (188dB)
but power higher at 291mW. Cost 292.27, slightly behind run 7's 292.18.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: whole-second timer, pairs label, progressive pair injection

- Timer now shows MM:SS (whole seconds, no decimal)
- Match counter label changed to "Pairs Matched: X/50"
- Add "New Words!" flash element to top bar
- When ≤10 unmatched pairs remain (and total < 50), inject 5 new
  pairs instantly; flash "New Words!" 3× in green; play ascending
  chime (C-E-G-C). Repeats until 50 total pairs matched.
- checkRoundComplete now fires at matchTarget (50) for normal rounds
- updateCardCount shows X/50 for normal matching rounds

commit.message:
Initial commit: FitMyTime Chrome Extension with full feature set and landing pages

Complete Chrome Extension for smart Google Calendar workout scheduling.
22 features across 3 tiers including smart scheduling, live countdown timer,
streak tracking, achievement badges, personal records, exercise swap,
mini calendar view, CSV export, OAuth auto-refresh, and custom exercises.
Includes landing page and Chrome Web Store listing page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

DART API ConnectionResetError 방지, 모멘텀 텍스트 포맷 개선, 전체 기능 업데이트

- DART API 호출에 dart_call_with_retry 래퍼 적용 (semaphore + exponential backoff)

- 글로벌 rate limiter: min_interval=1.0s, max_concurrent=1
- 모멘텀 텍스트: 항목별 줄바꿈 + 온점 종결 + blockquote 리스트 렌더링

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set price_per_task=0 so router matches all task budgets

Previous values (3 AET, 2.5 AET, 4 AET) exceeded the task budgets
posted by task_poster.py, causing the router to skip all candidates.
Zero means "accept any budget" per the router's matching logic.

commit.message:
Fix service URLs for management reporting and audit management

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 70-turn ablation analysis: bare Llama 3.1 8B vs Frank

Structured evaluation comparing the bare base model against Frank's full
architecture (same GPU, same model weights). 7 phases covering personality,
self-knowledge, theory of mind, multi-turn memory, hallucination resistance,
philosophy of mind, and meta-cognition. Methodology references Butlin et al.
(2023), Anthropic (2025), TMBench, and BeHonest.

Key findings: Frank 82% fewer deflections (2 vs 11), higher information
density (unique word ratio 0.929 vs 0.843), consistent response quality
across all 70 turns while bare model collapsed from 296 to 10 words/turn.

Supersedes CONVERSATION_DEEP_DIVE.md (preserved with redirect notice).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(erigon-extractor): all = phases 1-5 (snapshot + RPC); catalog: add Rust services + update scan roots

- erigon_extractor all: extend to run events+entity_flows+traces (phases 3-5 via RPC) in addition to headers+txs (phases 1-2 from snapshots)
- SYSTEMD_CATALOG_CANONICAL.yaml: replace infra/systemd/ → carene/ops/systemd-fedora/ scan root; add 12 new Rust service/timer entries (pq-btc-writer-*, pq-erigon-extractor, pq-lake-compact/retain, pq-rust-ingest-*)
